done_summary:
Triaged all 30 support tickets from `/input/repo/tickets.txt`. Produced:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with:
- All 30 tickets classified into one of 12 categories with urgency levels
- 2 duplicate clusters identified:
  - `[007, 011]` — Dashboard sharing permission bug (same account MC-3309, two reporters)
  - `[014, 023, 030]` — API rate limit blocking nightly ingestion (same sender, escalating follow-ups)
- 6 urgent tickets requiring immediate attention
- Summary counts by category and urgency
- Detailed notes explaining non-obvious classification decisions

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Human-readable summary with:
- Quick stats table
- Tickets organized by urgency tier (urgent → normal → low → spam)
- Duplicate cluster descriptions
- Recommended action order for the triager

**`/workspace/decisions/adr-001-through-005.md`** — ADRs for 5 non-obvious calls:
- API rate limits: bug-report vs billing-other
- Extortion email: spam vs security-incident
- Dashboard sharing: two tickets, one issue
- 500 errors: incident-active vs bug-report
- Downtime credit: billing-refund vs incident-active

Key findings: 4 spam/noise tickets, 2 active incidents, 1 security incident, 7 bug reports, 4 billing refund requests, and 2 duplicate clusters.
